brief_summary: Two-column ledger. Left (coral-filled, 'what it reverses'): bromine oxidation yellowing on white/off-white ABS, with the H2O2+UV mechanism noted. Right (dashed bordeaux with strikethroughs, 'what it doesn't fix'): polished shine from finger oils and abrasion, PBT discoloration, and dyed/colored ABS — each with a short note on why it falls outside the mechanism.
data_sources: retrobrighting-keycaps.mdx § 'What it doesn't fix' — 'Shine from finger oils is not a color problem ... That's a texture and gloss change, not a chemical one.'

retrobrighting-keycaps.mdx § 'What it doesn't fix' — 'PBT ... generally does not develop the same bromine-driven yellowing that vintage ABS does ... dousing PBT in peroxide and sunlight expecting an ABS-style reversal is treating the wrong diagnosis.'
retrobrighting-keycaps.mdx § 'What it doesn't fix' — 'Dyed or colored ABS is its own gamble too — the dye and the flame-retardant chemistry can interact with the peroxide unpredictably'
